perturbation.original_text: Consultant shall perform the following services: - The Consultant will provide the Company services as a Strategic Marketing & Fundraising Consultant. - The Consultant shall be responsible for the strategic planning of business expansion, including Fundraising and Stock Promotion, of the Company and its subsidiaries. - These Services shall include Marketing guidance and support, not limited to: ○ Graphics ○ Web ○ Social ○ Brand - These Services will include updates to investor decks, customer sales decks and other marketing material available to the public - The Company will provide the Consultant with the appropriate level of resources and information to perform such duties, and the Consultant shall be reimbursed for fees and expenses approved by the Company. - The Consultant will report directly to the CEO of the and will keep the CEO informed of all matters concerning the Services as requested by the CEO from time to time. - The Consultant acknowledges that he may be required to travel in order to provide the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant shall perform the following services: - The Contractor will provide the Company services as a Strategic Marketing & Fundraising Consultant. - The Contractor shall be responsible for the strategic planning of business expansion, including Fundraising and Stock Promotion, of the Company and its subsidiaries. - These Services shall include Marketing guidance and support, not limited to: ○ Graphics ○ Web ○ Social ○ Brand - These Services will include updates to investor decks, customer sales decks and other marketing material available to the public - The Company will provide the Consultant with the appropriate level of resources and information to perform such duties, and the Consultant shall be reimbursed for fees and expenses approved by the Company. - The Contractor will report directly to the CEO of the and will keep the CEO informed of all matters concerning the Services as requested by the CEO from time to time. - The Consultant acknowledges that he may be required to travel in order to provide the Services.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Consultant' to 'Contractor' in the first two sub-sections creates an in-text contradiction within Section 1.1. The section now refers to both 'Consultant' and 'Contractor' performing the same services, without defining if they are the same entity. This ambiguity could lead to disputes over who is responsible for which tasks, especially considering the last line still references 'Consultant'. This also contradicts with Section 1.2, which specify about the `Consultant` been paid, and it is unclear if it applies to the contractor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.  Services and Compensation. 1.1. Services. Consultant shall perform the following services: - The Contractor will provide the Company services as a Strategic Marketing & Fundraising Consultant. - The Contractor shall be responsible for the strategic planning of business expansion, including Fundraising and Stock Promotion, of the Company and its subsidiaries. - These Services shall include Marketing guidance and support, not limited to: ○ Graphics ○ Web ○ Social ○ Brand - These Services will include updates to investor decks, customer sales decks and other marketing material available to the public - The Company will provide the Consultant with the appropriate level of resources and information to perform such duties, and the Consultant shall be reimbursed for fees and expenses approved by the Company. - The Contractor will report directly to the CEO of the and will keep the CEO informed of all matters concerning the Services as requested by the CEO from time to time. - The Consultant acknowledges that he may be required to travel in order to provide the Services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Contractor' while retaining 'Consultant' within the same section (1.1), which also serves as the contradicted text. The original text consistently used 'Consultant.' The change creates ambiguity and a direct contradiction because it is unclear whether 'Consultant' and 'Contractor' are the same entity or two different roles with separate responsibilities, especially in the lines where both terms appear. This misalignment directly impacts Section 1.2 regarding compensation, as it becomes unclear whether the payment terms apply to both the 'Consultant' and the 'Contractor,' creating a strong in-text contradiction and potentially impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall reimburse Consultant, in accordance with Company policy, for all reasonable expenses incurred by Consultant in performing the Services pursuant to this Agreement, but only if Consultant receives written consent from an authorized agent of the Company prior to incurring such expenses and submits receipts for such expenses to the Company in accordance with the Company's general expense reimbursement policies.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company shall reimburse Consultant, in accordance with Company policy, for all reasonable expenses incurred by the Personnel in performing the Services pursuant to this Agreement, but only if Consultant receives written consent from an authorized agent of the Company prior to incurring such expenses and submits receipts for such expenses to the Company in accordance with the Company's general expense reimbursement policies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Consultant' to 'Personnel' in the reimbursement clause creates ambiguity. It's now unclear if 'Personnel' refers to the Consultant, employees of the Consultant, or another undefined group. This raises questions about whose expenses are covered and how the reimbursement policy applies to different individuals. This also opens up the possibility that Consultant's personal expenses are not covered and only personnel one's, contradiction the purpose of the Consultant reimbursement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 Expenses. The Company shall reimburse Consultant, in accordance with Company policy, for all reasonable expenses incurred by the Personnel in performing the Services pursuant to this Agreement, but only if Consultant receives written consent from an authorized agent of the Company prior to incurring such expenses and submits receipts for such expenses to the Company in accordance with the Company's general expense reimbursement policies.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Consultant' to 'Personnel' introduces ambiguity, as explained. However, the 'Contradicted Text' is identical to the 'Changed Text', indicating the change already exists in that section. While the change introduces potential confusion, it doesn't create a *direct* contradiction since the same language is used in both locations. The contract is ambiguous, but not self-contradictory based on the text provided.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant agrees to keep and maintain adequate, current, accurate, and authentic written records of all Inventions made by Consultant (solely or jointly with others) during the term of this Agreement, and for a period of three (3) years thereafter. The records will be in the form of notes, sketches, drawings, electronic files, reports, or any other format that is customary in the industry and/or otherwise specified by the Company. Such records are and remain the sole property of the Company at all times and upon Company's request, Consultant shall deliver (or cause to be delivered) the same.
perturbation.changed_text: TruckThat LLC agrees to keep and maintain adequate, current, accurate, and authentic written records of all Inventions made by Consultant (solely or jointly with others) during the term of this Agreement, and for a period of three (3) years thereafter. The records will be in the form of notes, sketches, drawings, electronic files, reports, or any other format that is customary in the industry and/or otherwise specified by the Company. Such records are and remain the sole property of the Company at all times and upon Company's request, Consultant shall deliver (or cause to be delivered) the same.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Consultant agrees' to 'TruckThat LLC agrees' blurs the lines of individual versus company responsibility. While TruckThat LLC is identified as the Consultant, this phrasing suggests the LLC itself, rather than the individual performing the services, is responsible for record-keeping. This contradicts the reference to 'inventions made by Consultant,' as inventions are typically attributed to individuals. Now the entity, TruckThat LLC, is bound to the duty to keep the records, even when is the `Consultant` the onw who is performing the duties and creating the inventions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4. Maintenance of Records. TruckThat LLC agrees to keep and maintain adequate, current, accurate, and authentic written records of all Inventions made by Consultant (solely or jointly with others) during the term of this Agreement, and for a period of three (3) years thereafter. The records will be in the form of notes, sketches, drawings, electronic files, reports, or any other format that is customary in the industry and/or otherwise specified by the Company. Such records are and remain the sole property of the Company at all times and upon Company's request, Consultant shall deliver (or cause to be delivered) the same.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Consultant agrees' to 'TruckThat LLC agrees' introduces ambiguity regarding who is responsible for maintaining records of inventions. While TruckThat LLC is identified as the Consultant, assigning the record-keeping duty to the LLC rather than the individual inventor creates a contradiction. The original text implicitly assumes the individual consultant creates the inventions and thus maintains the records, as inventions are attributable to people. The Changed Text and the Contradicted text are exactly the same which still causes the same contradiction because both point to the LLC being the one doing the record keeping when in fact it is the `Consultant` doing so.
